verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A (Hsp70) is canonically a cytosolic chaperone, but it is well-documented as a tumor-cell surface marker: stressed and transformed cells display Hsp70 on the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane (membrane Hsp70 / memHsp70), recognized by the clinical-stage cmHsp70.1 antibody and exploited by NK-cell-targeting strategies (e.g., TKD peptide). Surface Hsp70 is also externalized during immunogenic cell death and stress. Mechanism is non-classical (lipid binding to outer-leaflet phosphatidylserine / Bag-mediated translocation), so this fits cell-state-induced ecto-display rather than a baseline anchor.
